attractions.name: Jungfraujoch
attractions.description: The 'Top of Europe' featuring an observation terrace and ice palace.

attractions.name: Matterhorn
attractions.description: Iconic pyramid-shaped mountain peak on the border between Switzerland and Italy.

attractions.name: Mount Pilatus
attractions.description: Mountain massif overlooking Lucerne, accessible by the world's steepest cogwheel railway.

attractions.name: Aletsch Glacier
attractions.description: The largest glacier in the Alps, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

attractions.name: Chillon Castle
attractions.description: An island castle located on Lake Geneva, near Montreux.

attractions.name: Lauterbrunnen Valley
attractions.description: A stunning valley known for its 72 waterfalls and dramatic cliffs.

attractions.name: St. Moritz
attractions.description: Luxury alpine resort town known for winter sports and high-end lifestyle.

attractions.name: Gornergrat Railway
attractions.description: A rack railway that offers spectacular views of the Matterhorn and surrounding glaciers.

restaurants.name: Chez Vrony
restaurants.description: Famous mountain restaurant in Zermatt with organic local food and Matterhorn views.

restaurants.name: Restaurant 1865
restaurants.description: Fine dining in the heart of the Alps, focusing on Swiss culinary traditions.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Bellevue
restaurants.description: Traditional Swiss cuisine with a focus on fondue and raclette.

restaurants.name: Le Chalet
restaurants.description: Cozy atmosphere serving authentic alpine dishes in a rustic setting.

hidden_gems.name: Valle Verzasca
hidden_gems.description: A hidden valley with crystal clear emerald water and ancient stone bridges.

nightlife.name: Papperla Pub
nightlife.description: The go-to spot for après-ski in Zermatt with live music.

shopping.name: Bahnhofstrasse Zermatt
shopping.description: The main street for luxury watches, Swiss chocolates, and high-end outdoor gear.

festivals.name: Montreux Jazz Festival
festivals.description: World-renowned music festival held annually on the shores of Lake Geneva.

adventure_activities.name: Paragliding in Interlaken
adventure_activities.description: Experience the Alps from the sky with tandem paragliding flights.

local_tips: Purchase a Swiss Travel Pass for unlimited travel on trains, buses, and boats.
Tap water in the Alps is among the purest in the world; bring a reusable bottle.
Always check weather forecasts before hiking, as conditions change rapidly.
Tipping is not mandatory but rounding up the bill is appreciated.

safety.general: Switzerland is extremely safe, but mountain environments require caution.
safety.water_safety: Avoid drinking from glacial streams unless marked as potable.
safety.transport_safety: Trains are punctual and safe; always validate your ticket if required.

culture.overview: Swiss culture is a blend of German, French, and Italian influences with a strong emphasis on punctuality and respect for nature.
culture.etiquette: Greet people with a polite 'Grüezi' (in German-speaking regions) and keep noise levels low in public spaces.

transport.getting_around: The Swiss rail network (SBB) is the most efficient way to travel between mountain towns.
transport.airport: Zurich Airport (ZRH) and Geneva Airport (GVA) are the primary gateways.

visa_information.domestic_indian_travellers: Requires a Schengen Visa for entry into Switzerland.
visa_information.foreign_nationals: Check local embassy requirements for Schengen area access.

weather.best_time_to_visit: June to September for hiking; December to March for skiing.
weather.avoid: November and April (shoulder seasons) when many mountain lifts are closed for maintenance.
